commands.review_summary:
The PR changes exec loop-detection hashing so completed exec output remains part of the fingerprint while failed exec results hash only stable status, exitCode, and timedOut fields, with regression coverage for failed and completed paths.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +123. Total +137 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main hashes failed exec output into resultHash while getNoProgressStreak stops on hash drift; this review did not run a live replay because the checkout is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer availability-risk review are required; there is no narrow automated code repair identified right now.

target.title: fix: strip volatile output from exec result hash to fix no-progress detection (#93917)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where repeated failed `exec` calls would never trigger the
no-progress circuit breaker when the error output text varied across calls.
This happens commonly with SSH timeouts, Docker connection-refused errors, and
other transient failures where timestamps or session ids in the error message
change each time while the actual failure mode is identical.

Fixes #93917

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts:213-230` | Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `completed` keeps output in hash, `failed` excludes volatile output text — only `{status, exitCode, timedOut}` is hashed |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts:669-697` | Update failed exec test: repeated failed calls with varying text now correctly escalate to `global_circuit_breaker` |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection-evidence.test.ts` | Standalone evidence test — Docker `connection refused` loop + `date` varying-output loop, exercising production code path |

## Why This Change Was Made

Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `failed` exec results are hashed without the
volatile output text, while `completed` exec results keep output in the hash
as a real progress signal. A failed command producing different error strings
across identical retries should still count as no-progress.

## User Impact

Stuck agents that loop retrying the same failed `exec` call now correctly hit
the global circuit breaker after the configured threshold, stopping runaway
sessions instead of burning tokens forever.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof (standalone evidence test)

`src/agents/tool-loop-detection-evidence.test.ts` exercises both paths with
realistic payloads — Docker `connection refused` for failed exec, `date` with
varying timestamps for completed exec — verifying the fix end-to-end through
the production `detectToolCallLoop` code path:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/agents/tool-loop-detection-evidence.test.ts \
    src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ #93917 real behavior proof > escalates repeated docker-failure exec calls
   to the global circuit breaker despite varying error text
 ✓ #93917 real behavior proof > keeps completed exec with varying output
   at warning (output is real progress)
 ✓ tool-loop-detection > keeps changing exec output below the global
   no-progress breaker
 ✓ tool-loop-detection > escalates repeated failed exec calls to the
   global breaker when status and exitCode are stable
 ...
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  61 passed (61)
```

### Scenario walkthrough

**Scenario A — Docker failure loop (before fix → after fix):**
```
before: 30 "docker ps" calls, each "connection refused (attempt N)"
        → noProgressStreak always 1 (error text varies by attempt number)
        → agent burns tokens forever, never hits circuit breaker

after:  30 "docker ps" calls, each "connection refused (attempt N)"
        → noProgressStreak reaches 30 ({status:failed, exitCode:1, timedOut:false} stable)
        → global_circuit_breaker triggers, stops runaway session
```

**Scenario B — `date` in a loop (before fix → after fix, unchanged):**
```
before: 30 "date" calls, each returns different timestamp
        → noProgressStreak stays at 1 (completed output varies as expected)
        → stuck at warning level only — output IS real progress

after:  same behavior — completed output intentionally kept in hash,
        varying completed output remains a real progress signal
```

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
